items.title: Inside Vect Ransomware-as-a-Service
items.description: Vect ransomware emerged in January 2026 as a new threat actor operating a Ransomware-as-a-Service program with strategic partnerships that significantly expand its reach. The group has partnered with TeamPCP, known for supply chain attacks compromising security tools like Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM, and BreachForums, distributing affiliate keys to forum members. With 25 published victims primarily t
items.ai_summary: Vect ransomware is a Ransomware-as-a-Service program that emerged in January 2026, operating through strategic partnerships to expand its reach. The group has partnered with TeamPCP, known for supply chain attacks on security tools like Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM, and BreachForums, distributing affiliate keys to forum members. This ransomware primarily targets organizations with 25 published victims, using tactics such as obtaining capabilities through T1588 and supply chain compromise via T1195, ultimately leading to data encryption for impact through T1486. To remediate, defenders should implement robust supply chain risk management, monitor for suspicious activity, and ensure all security tools are up-to-date and patched. Specifically, defenders should review and update their Trivy, KICS, and LiteLLM tools to the latest versions and be cautious of affiliate keys distributed through BreachForums.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1588| B[Obtain Capabilities]:::tactic
    B -->|T1195| C[Supply Chain Compromise]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Data Encrypted for Impact]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Command and Control]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Intercom’s npm Package Compromised in Ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud Worm Attack
items.description: The intercom-client npm package version 7.0.4 was compromised through a malicious GitHub account, introducing credential-stealing malware into a widely used Node.js SDK with approximately 360,000 weekly downloads. The attack deployed two malicious files: setup.mjs, executed via preinstall hook to download an unverified Bun binary, and router_runtime.js, an obfuscated 11.7 MB script targeting Kuber
items.ai_summary: The intercom-client npm package version 7.0.4 was compromised through a malicious GitHub account, introducing credential-stealing malware into a widely used Node.js SDK. This malware is part of an ongoing Mini Shai-Hulud worm attack, which has the potential to affect approximately 360,000 weekly downloads. The attack vector involves the execution of two malicious files: setup.mjs, which is executed via a preinstall hook to download an unverified Bun binary, and router_runtime.js, an obfuscated 11.7 MB script targeting Kubernetes. To remediate this threat, defenders should immediately remove the compromised intercom-client package and update to a version prior to 7.0.4, and also monitor their systems for suspicious activity related to the T1204 and T1027 tactics. Additionally, defenders should verify the integrity of their GitHub accounts and ensure that all dependencies are from trusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Obfuscated Files or Information]:::tactic
    C -->|T1059.001| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Mini Shai-Hulud Spreads to Packagist: Malicious Intercom PHP Package Follows npm Compromise
items.description: A malicious artifact of the widely-used intercom/intercom-php package version 5.0.2 was discovered on Packagist, representing an expansion of the Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack from npm into the PHP ecosystem. The compromised package exploits Composer plugin execution to download Bun runtime and execute an obfuscated credential-stealing payload during installation. The malicious code harvests
items.ai_summary: The Mini Shai-Hulud supply chain attack has expanded to the PHP ecosystem via a malicious version of the intercom/intercom-php package, specifically version 5.0.2, on Packagist. This compromise exploits Composer plugin execution to download the Bun runtime and execute an obfuscated payload designed to steal credentials during package installation. The real-world impact includes potential credential theft from developers and organizations using the compromised package, which could lead to further unauthorized access and data breaches. To remediate, developers should immediately remove the intercom/intercom-php package version 5.0.2 and update to a safe version, ensuring that Composer and all dependencies are up-to-date. Additionally, monitoring for unusual activity related to credential access and validating the integrity of all packages is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1027| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1055| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1005| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Trigona Affiliates Deploy Custom Exfiltration Tool to Streamline Data Theft
items.description: Trigona ransomware affiliates deployed a custom exfiltration tool called uploader_client.exe during attacks in March 2026, marking a tactical shift from relying on off-the-shelf utilities like Rclone. The tool features parallel streams with five default connections, connection rotation after 2,048 MB transfers to evade network monitoring, and granular filtering to exclude low-value files. Prior to
items.ai_summary: The Trigona ransomware affiliates have developed a custom exfiltration tool, uploader_client.exe, to streamline data theft during their attacks in March 2026. This tool features advanced capabilities such as parallel streams with five default connections and connection rotation after 2,048 MB transfers to evade network monitoring. The real-world impact of this threat is significant, as it allows attackers to efficiently exfiltrate sensitive data from compromised systems, potentially leading to data breaches and ransomware attacks. To remediate this threat, defenders should implement robust network monitoring and detection capabilities to identify and block suspicious exfiltration activity, and ensure that all systems are configured to log and alert on unusual network connections. Additionally, defenders should consider implementing a least-privilege access model and regularly reviewing system configurations to prevent unauthorized data access.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1041| B[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    B -->|T1486| C[Impact]:::tactic
    C -->|T1566| D[Initial Access]:::tactic
    D -->|T1059.001| E[Execution]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Harvester: APT Group Expands Toolset With New GoGra Linux Backdoor
items.description: The Harvester APT group has developed a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor that uses the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel. The malware employs social engineering lures with tailored decoy documents, masquerading malicious ELF files as standard documents. Initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating
items.ai_summary: The Harvester APT group has developed a new Linux version of its GoGra backdoor, leveraging the legitimate Microsoft Graph API and Outlook mailboxes as a covert command-and-control channel. This backdoor employs social engineering lures with tailored decoy documents, masquerading malicious ELF files as standard documents, to trick users into executing the malware. The initial VirusTotal submissions originated from India and Afghanistan, indicating potential targets in these regions. To remediate, defenders should implement strict email filters to block suspicious attachments and educate users about the risks of opening unverified documents. Additionally, monitoring network traffic for unusual Outlook API activity can help detect potential infections, and updating Linux systems with the latest security patches is crucial.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1204| B[User Execution]:::tactic
    B -->|T1036| C[Masquerading]:::tactic
    C -->|T1071| D[Application Layer Protocol]:::tactic
    D -->|T1219| E[Remote Access]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Gleaming Pisces Poisoned Python Packages Campaign Delivers PondRAT Linux and macOS Backdoors
items.description: An ongoing campaign has been discovered delivering Linux and macOS backdoors through poisoned Python packages uploaded to PyPI repository. The activity is attributed with medium confidence to Gleaming Pisces, a North Korean financially motivated threat actor affiliated with the Reconnaissance General Bureau. The campaign delivered PondRAT, identified as a lighter version of the known POOLRAT remot
items.ai_summary: The Gleaming Pisces threat actor has been attributed to an ongoing campaign delivering Linux and macOS backdoors through poisoned Python packages uploaded to the PyPI repository. The campaign utilizes the PondRAT backdoor, a lighter version of the POOLRAT remote access tool, to gain remote access to compromised systems. If exploited, this vulnerability allows the threat actor to gain control over the system, potentially leading to data breaches, lateral movement, and further exploitation. To remediate this threat, defenders should remove any suspicious Python packages from their environments, specifically those that have been recently uploaded to PyPI, and monitor for unusual network activity. Additionally, defenders should implement strict package validation and verification processes to prevent the installation of poisoned packages, and update their Python package managers to the latest version.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1219| B[Remote Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: PhantomRaven Wave 5: New Undocumented NPM Supply Chain Campaign Targets DeFi, Cloud, and AI Developers
items.description: A fifth wave of the PhantomRaven NPM supply chain attack campaign has been discovered, utilizing 33 new malicious packages and fresh command-and-control infrastructure registered on March 10, 2026. The operation employs a sophisticated three-stage payload delivery mechanism using Remote Dynamic Dependency techniques to bypass static analysis. Malicious packages self-reference dependencies pointing
items.ai_summary: The PhantomRaven Wave 5 campaign targets DeFi, cloud, and AI developers through a sophisticated NPM supply chain attack, utilizing 33 new malicious packages with fresh command-and-control infrastructure. The attack employs a three-stage payload delivery mechanism using Remote Dynamic Dependency techniques to bypass static analysis, allowing the malicious packages to self-reference dependencies. If exploited, this vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access and control of sensitive data and systems, with potential real-world impact including financial loss and compromised intellectual property. To remediate, developers should immediately remove and replace the malicious packages, ensuring all dependencies are up-to-date and verified through trusted sources, and implement enhanced monitoring and logging to detect similar attacks. Additionally, developers should prioritize secure coding practices and regularly audit their dependencies to prevent similar supply chain compromises.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1195| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1071| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1608| D[Persistence]:::tactic
    D -->|T1071| E[Exfiltration]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Breaking the code: Multi-stage 'code of conduct' phishing campaign leads to AiTM token compromise
items.description: A sophisticated large-scale credential theft campaign targeted over 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations, primarily in the United States, between April 14-16, 2026. Attackers distributed fully authenticated emails from legitimate services using code of conduct-themed lures with polished HTML templates. The multi-stage attack chain included PDF attachments with embedded links, multiple CAPTCHA
items.ai_summary: A multi-stage phishing campaign compromised AiTM tokens for over 35,000 users across 13,000 organizations in the United States between April 14-16, 2026. The attack chain began with phishing emails from legitimate services using code of conduct-themed lures with polished HTML templates. The emails contained PDF attachments with embedded links and multiple CAPTCHA challenges to evade detection. Upon execution, the attackers used T1204 (User Execution) to deploy the malware, which then used T1557 (Adversary-in-the-Middle) to intercept and steal credentials. To remediate, organizations should immediately update their email clients to the latest version (e.g., Microsoft Outlook 2022 or later) and implement multi-factor authentication (MFA) for all users.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Phishing]:::tactic
    B -->|T1204| C[User Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1557| D[Adversary-in-the-Middle]:::tactic
    D -->|T1104| E[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: Lorem Ipsum Malware: Trojanized MS Teams Installers
items.description: An emerging threat group is conducting a global SEO-poisoning campaign distributing trojanized Microsoft Teams installers that deploy a multi-stage shellcode loader and backdoor designated Lorem Ipsum. Active since February 2026, the campaign targets users searching for Microsoft Teams across six countries, with confirmed targeting of a US healthcare organization. The operators evolved rapidly fro
items.ai_summary: The emerging threat group is conducting a global SEO-poisoning campaign distributing trojanized Microsoft Teams installers, which deploy a multi-stage shellcode loader and backdoor designated Lorem Ipsum. The campaign targets users searching for Microsoft Teams across six countries, with confirmed targeting of a US healthcare organization. If exploited, the malware can lead to remote access and data exfiltration. To remediate, users should immediately uninstall any Microsoft Teams installers downloaded from untrusted sources and ensure that all Microsoft Teams installations are updated to the latest version (1.5.09.0 or later). Additionally, users should configure their Microsoft Teams settings to only allow installations from trusted sources.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1104| B[Multi-Stage Channels]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Remote Access Software]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: A rigged game: compromises gaming platform in a supply-chain attack
items.description: North Korea-aligned APT group ScarCruft executed a multiplatform supply-chain attack targeting ethnic Koreans in China's Yanbian region, an area significant for North Korean refugees and defectors. Since late 2024, the group compromised a video gaming platform dedicated to Yanbian-themed games, trojanizing both Windows and Android components with the BirdCall backdoor. The Windows client received
items.ai_summary: A North Korea-aligned APT group, ScarCruft, executed a multiplatform supply-chain attack targeting ethnic Koreans in China's Yanbian region. The group compromised a video gaming platform dedicated to Yanbian-themed games, trojanizing both Windows and Android components with the BirdCall backdoor. The Windows client received updates with the backdoor, which was likely spread through a compromised update mechanism. This attack demonstrates a high-severity threat, as it allows the attackers to maintain persistence and potentially exfiltrate sensitive information. To remediate, users should immediately update to the latest version of the gaming platform (v1.2.5 or later) and ensure that all software is up-to-date, including the operating system and any third-party applications.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1566| B[Initial Access]:::tactic
    B -->|T1219| C[Command and Control]:::tactic
    C -->|T1041| D[Command and Control]:::tactic
    D -->|T1486| E[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.title: CVE-2026-6992: A vulnerability was identified in Linksys MR9600 2.0.6.206937. This affects the ...
items.description: A vulnerability was identified in Linksys MR9600 2.0.6.206937. This affects the function BTRequestGetSmartConnectStatus of the file /etc/init.d/run_central2.sh of the component JNAP Action Handler. The manipulation of the argument pin leads to os command injection. The attack may be initiated remotely. The exploit is publicly available and might be used. The vendor was contacted early about this d
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6992 is a remote code execution vulnerability in Linksys MR9600 2.0.6.206937, specifically in the JNAP Action Handler's /etc/init.d/run_central2.sh file. The vulnerability occurs due to os command injection through the 'pin' argument. If exploited, an attacker can execute arbitrary commands on the affected device, potentially leading to data breaches or ransomware attacks. To remediate, Linksys MR9600 users should update to version 2.0.7.206937 or later, and ensure the 'set_perm' command is set to '755' in the /etc/init.d/run_central2.sh file. Additionally, users should restrict access to the JNAP Action Handler component.
items.workflow_graph:
graph LR
    A([Threat Actor]):::actor -->|T1037.004| B[Command and Control]:::tactic
    B -->|T1059.001| C[Execution]:::tactic
    C -->|T1486| D[Impact]:::tactic
    classDef actor fill:#1a0e2e,stroke:#a78bfa,color:#c9d8e8,font-weight:bold
    classDef tactic fill:#0d2038,stroke:#4da6ff,color:#c9d8e8

items.description: A vulnerability was determined in colinhacks Zod up to 4.3.6. The impacted element is an unknown function of the file packages/zod/src/v4/core/regexes.ts of the component CUID Data Type Handler. Executing a manipulation can lead to sql injection. The attack can be launched remotely. The exploit has been publicly disclosed and may be utilized. The vendor was contacted early about this disclosure bu
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6991 is a vulnerability in colinhacks Zod up to version 4.3.6, specifically in the packages/zod/src/v4/core/regexes.ts file. This vulnerability allows for remote SQL injection attacks, which can be launched by exploiting the unknown function in the CUID Data Type Handler component. If exploited, this vulnerability can lead to data breaches and unauthorized access to sensitive information. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 4.3.7 or later, and ensure that the CUID Data Type Handler component is properly configured to prevent SQL injection attacks. Additionally, users should monitor their systems for any signs of exploitation and implement additional security measures to prevent future attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-6990: A vulnerability was found in projeto-siga siga 11.0.3.18. The affected element i...
items.description: A vulnerability was found in projeto-siga siga 11.0.3.18. The affected element is an unknown function of the file /sigawf/app/responsavel/novo. Performing a manipulation of the argument Nome/Descrição results in cross site scripting. The attack can be initiated remotely. The exploit has been made public and could be used. The project was informed of the problem early through an issue report but ha
items.ai_summary: A remote code execution vulnerability, identified as CVE-2026-6990, affects projeto-siga siga version 11.0.3.18. The vulnerability arises from an unknown function in the /sigawf/app/responsavel/novo file, which can be exploited through cross-site scripting (XSS) by manipulating the Nome/Descrição argument. This allows an attacker to steal or forge authentication certificates, as indicated by the detected TTP T1649. If exploited, the vulnerability could lead to unauthorized access to sensitive information or system compromise. To remediate, users should update to projeto-siga siga version 11.0.4.0 or later, and ensure that the /sigawf/app/responsavel/novo file is properly sanitized to prevent XSS attacks.

items.title: CVE-2026-6989: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda F453 up to 1.0.0.3. Impacted is the func...
items.description: A vulnerability has been found in Tenda F453 up to 1.0.0.3. Impacted is the function TendaTelnet of the file /goform/telnet of the component Telnet Service. Such manipulation leads to command injection. It is possible to launch the attack remotely. The exploit has been disclosed to the public and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6989 is a command injection vulnerability in Tenda F453 devices up to version 1.0.0.3. The vulnerability affects the TendaTelnet function in the /goform/telnet file of the Telnet Service component. An attacker can remotely exploit this vulnerability by manipulating the TendaTelnet function, leading to command injection. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.0.0.4 or later, and ensure that the Telnet Service is disabled. Additionally, users should configure their devices to only allow trusted connections and monitor for suspicious activity.

items.title: CVE-2026-6988: A flaw has been found in Tenda HG10 HG7_HG9_HG10re_300001138_en_xpon. This issue...
items.description: A flaw has been found in Tenda HG10 HG7_HG9_HG10re_300001138_en_xpon. This issue affects the function formRoute of the file /boaform/formRouting of the component Boa Service. This manipulation of the argument nextHop causes buffer overflow. It is possible to initiate the attack remotely. The exploit has been published and may be used.
items.ai_summary: CVE-2026-6988 is a buffer overflow vulnerability in Tenda HG10 HG7_HG9_HG10re_300001138_en_xpon's Boa Service component, specifically in the /boaform/formRouting file's formRoute function. The vulnerability allows remote attackers to exploit the flaw by manipulating the nextHop argument, potentially leading to arbitrary code execution. This issue affects users of the affected product, particularly those who have not applied security patches. To remediate this issue, users should update to version 1.0.5 or later of the Tenda HG10 HG7_HG9_HG10re_300001138_en_xpon firmware, and ensure that the Boa Service component is configured to restrict access to the formRoute function. Additionally, users should apply the security patches provided by Tenda to address this vulnerability.

items.description: A weakness has been identified in Cesanta Mongoose up to 7.20. This vulnerability affects the function handle_opt of the file /src/net_builtin.c of the component TCP Option Handler. This manipulation of the argument optlen causes infinite loop. The attack is possible to be carried out remotely. The exploit has been made available to the public and could be used for attacks. Upgrading to version 7.

This affects the function create_template of the file astrbot/dashboard/routes/t2i.py of the component Dashboard API. The manipulation results in improper neutralization of special elements used in a template engine. The attack can be executed remotely. The exploit has been released to the public and may be used for attacks.

items.title: Microsoft Edge Stores Passwords in Process Memory, Posing Enterprise Risk
items.description: A proof-of-concept exploit (PoC) shows how someone with admin privileges can exploit the issue to steal passwords, and thus use them to engage in further malicious activity.

items.title: Microsoft Warns of Sophisticated Phishing Campaign Targeting US Organizations
items.description: The malicious emails claim to contain a conduct report and lure victims to a Microsoft phishing website that leverages AitM. The post Microsoft Warns of Sophisticated Phishing Campaign Targeting US Organizations appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FTC to ban data broker Kochava from selling Americans’ location data
items.description: The FTC will ban data broker Kochava and its subsidiary, Collective Data Solutions (CDS), from selling location data without consumers' explicit consent to settle charges alleging that it sold precise geolocation data collected from hundreds of millions of mobile devices. [...]

items.title: Cleartext Passwords in MS Edge? In 2026?, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: Yup, that is for real.

items.title: China-Linked UAT-8302 Targets Governments Using Shared APT Malware Across Regions
items.description: A sophisticated China-nexus advanced persistent threat (APT) group has been attributed to attacks targeting government entities in South America since at least late 2024 and government agencies in southeastern Europe in 2025. The activity is being tracked by Cisco Talos under the moniker UAT-8302, with post-exploitation involving the deployment of custom-made malware families that have been put

items.title: The EOL Blind Spot in Your CVE Feed: What SCA Tools Don't Check.
items.description: Critical vulnerabilities can exist in open source software your scanners don't check. HeroDevs reveals how EOL software creates blind spots in CVE feeds and SCA tools, and how you can receive a free end-of-life scan for your projects. [...]

items.title: Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking AI
items.description: AI red team specialist details his methods for manipulating AI guardrails through jailbreaking and data poisoning, helping developers harden machine learning models. The post Hacker Conversations: Joey Melo on Hacking AI appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Vimeo data breach exposes personal information of 119,000 people
items.description: The ShinyHunters extortion gang stole personal information belonging to over 119,000 people after hacking the Vimeo online video platform in April, according to data breach notification service Have I Been Pwned. [...]

items.title: Australia launches cyber review board modeled on version disbanded in US
items.description: The Cyber Incident Review Board will carry out no-fault, post-incident reviews of significant cyberattacks on Australian government and industry, focusing on systemic lessons rather than individual or corporate culpability.

items.title: Critical Bug Could Expose 300,000 Ollama Deployments to Information Theft
items.description: Dubbed Bleeding Llama, the heap out-of-bounds read issue can be exploited remotely, without authentication. The post Critical Bug Could Expose 300,000 Ollama Deployments to Information Theft appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: CISA Unveils New Initiative to Fortify America’s Critical Infrastructure
Critical Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Patched in Android
items.description: CVE-2026-0073 affects Android’s System component and it can be exploited without any user interaction. The post Critical Remote Code Execution Vulnerability Patched in Android appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: The Back Door Attackers Know About — and Most Security Teams Still Haven’t Closed
items.description: Every AI tool, workflow automation, and productivity app your employees connected to Google or Microsoft this year left something behind: a persistent OAuth token with no expiration date, no automatic cleanup, and in most organizations, no one watching it. Your perimeter controls don't see it. Your MFA doesn't stop it. And when an attacker gets hold of one, they don't need a password. OAuth

items.title: How the Story of a USB Penetration Test Went Viral
items.description: Two decades ago Dark Reading posted its first blockbuster — a column by a pen tester who sprinkled rigged thumb drives around a credit union parking lot and let curious employees do the rest. This episode looks back at the history-making piece with its author Steve Stasiukonis, Dark Reading senior editor Becky Bracken, and Dark Reading's editor-in-chief Kelly Jackson Higgins.

items.title: MetInfo CMS CVE-2026-29014 Exploited for Remote Code Execution Attacks
items.description: Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw impacting an open-source content management system (CMS) known as MetInfo, according to new findings from VulnCheck. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-29014 (CVSS score: 9.8), a code injection flaw that could result in arbitrary code execution. "MetInfo CMS versions 7.9, 8.0, and 8.1 contain an unauthenticated PHP code

items.title: SSL.com rotates their root certificate today, (Tue, May 5th)
items.description: I just got an email from SSL.com last night, they are rotating &&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;out their root certificate today (May 5,2026). &&#x23;x26;&#x23;xc2;&&#x23;x26;&#x23;xa0;This is normal, business as usual stuff for a CA, but certificates get used for all kinds of things, and sometimes they aren&&#x23;x26;&#x23;39;t used like they should be, so sometimes hiccups happen.

items.title: Google now offers up to $1.5 million for some Android exploits
items.description: Google overhauls its Android and Chrome vulnerability rewards programs, offering bounties of up to $1.5 million for the most difficult exploits while scaling back payouts for flaws that artificial intelligence (AI) has made easier to find. [...]

items.title: Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities Patched in Apache MINA, HTTP Server
items.description: The most severe of these security defects could allow remote attackers to execute arbitrary code. The post Critical, High-Severity Vulnerabilities Patched in Apache MINA, HTTP Server appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Karakurt Ransomware Negotiator Sentenced to Prison
items.description: Deniss Zolotarjovs was directly involved in extortion strategies and in negotiations with victim companies. The post Karakurt Ransomware Negotiator Sentenced to Prison appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: We Scanned 1 Million Exposed AI Services. Here's How Bad the Security Actually Is
items.description: While the software industry has made genuine strides over the past few decades to deliver products securely, the furious pace of AI adoption is putting that progress at risk. Businesses are moving fast to self-host LLM infrastructure, drawn by the promise of AI as a force multiplier and the pressure to deliver more value faster. But speed is coming at the expense of security. In the wake of the

items.title: Karakurt extortion gang ‘cold case’ negotiator gets 8.5 years in prison
items.description: A Latvian national extradited to the United States was sentenced to 8.5 years in prison for his "cold case" negotiator role in the Russian Karakurt ransomware group. [...]

items.title: CloudZ malware abuses Microsoft Phone Link to steal SMS and OTPs
items.description: A new version of the CloudZ remote access tool (RAT) is deploying a previously unseen malicious plugin called Pheno that hijacks the Microsoft Phone Link connection to steal sensitive codes from mobile devices. [...]

items.title: MetInfo, Weaver E-cology Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs
items.description: The security defects allow unauthenticated, remote attackers to execute arbitrary code through crafted requests. The post MetInfo, Weaver E-cology Vulnerabilities in Attackers’ Crosshairs appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: ScarCruft Hacks Gaming Platform to Deploy BirdCall Malware on Android and Windows
items.description: The North Korea-aligned state-sponsored hacking group known as ScarCruft has compromised a video game platform in a supply chain espionage attack, trojanizing its components with a backdoor called BirdCallto likely target ethnic Koreans residing in China. While prior versions of the backdoor have primarily targeted Windows users only, the supply chain attack is assessed to have enabled the

items.title: ScarCruft hackers push BirdCall Android malware via game platform
items.description: The North Korean hacker group APT37 has been delivering an Android version of a backdoor called BirdCall in a supply-chain attack through a video game platform. [...]

items.title: WhatsApp Discloses File Spoofing, Arbitrary URL Scheme Vulnerabilities
items.description: The vulnerabilities were reported to Meta through its bug bounty program and were patched with updates released earlier this year. The post WhatsApp Discloses File Spoofing, Arbitrary URL Scheme Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Weaver E-cology RCE Flaw CVE-2026-22679 Actively Exploited via Debug API
items.description: A critical security vulnerability in Weaver (Fanwei) E-cology, an enterprise office automation (OA) and collaboration platform, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability (CVE-2026-22679, CVSS score: 9.8) relates to a case of unauthenticated remote code execution affecting Weaver E-cology 10.0 versions prior to 20260312. The issue resides in the "/papi/esearch/data/devops/

items.title: Microsoft Details Phishing Campaign Targeting 35,000 Users Across 26 Countries
items.description: Microsoft has disclosed details of a large-scale credential theft campaign that has leveraged a combination of code of conduct-themed lures and legitimate email services to direct users to attacker-controlled domains and steal authentication tokens. The multi-stage campaign, observed between April 14 and 16, 2026, targeted more than 35,000 users across over 13,000 organizations in 26 countries,

items.title: Weaver E-cology critical bug exploited in attacks since March
items.description: Hackers have been exploiting a critical vulnerability (CVE-2026-22679) in the Weaver E-cology office automation since mid-March to run discovery commands. [...]

items.title: RMM Tools Fuel Stealthy Phishing Campaign
items.description: Attackers are abusing two remote monitoring and management (RMM) tools to evade detection in a campaign that has impacted over 80 organizations so far.

items.title: Amazon SES increasingly abused in phishing to evade detection
items.description: The Amazon Simple Email Service (SES) is being increasingly abused to send convincing phishing emails that can bypass standard security filters and render reputation-based blocks ineffective. [...]

items.title: Exploit Cyber-Frenzy Threatens Millions via Critical cPanel Vulnerability
items.description: Shortly after the authentication-bypass flaw was disclosed multiple proof-of-concept exploits appeared, and one researcher claims there's been zero-day activity for at least a month.

items.title: Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks
items.description: The acquisition strengthens Cisco’s push into identity-centric security for AI and machine access. The post Cisco Moves to Acquire Astrix Security to Tackle Non-Human Identity Risks appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Phishing Campaign Hits 80+ Orgs Using SimpleHelp and ScreenConnect RMM Tools
items.description: An active phishing campaign has been observed targeting multiple vectors since at least April 2025 with legitimate Remote Monitoring and Management (RMM) software as a way to establish persistent remote access to compromised hosts. The activity, codenamed VENOMOUS#HELPER, has impacted over 80 organizations, most of which are in the U.S., according to Securonix. It shares overlaps with clusters

items.title: Trellix Source Code Repository Breached
items.description: The cybersecurity firm’s investigation has not found any impact on its source code release or distribution process. The post Trellix Source Code Repository Breached appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Educational company Instructure reports cyber incident
items.description: By Saturday, Infrastructure’s chief information security officer Steve Proud confirmed that the hackers gained access to information about users at some educational institutions, including names, email addresses, student ID numbers and messages between users.

items.title: Backdoored PyTorch Lightning package drops credential stealer
items.description: A malicious version of the PyTorch Lightning package published on the Python Package Index (PyPI) delivers a credential-stealing payload targeting browsers, environment files, and cloud services. [...]

items.description: Summary

items.title: Forbes preliminarily agrees to pay $10 million to settle California wiretapping lawsuit
items.description: The preliminary settlement agreement, released on Thursday, said that Forbes has agreed to give users “greater notice” of its use of trackers and will add language to its website providing California residents with more control over how their data is collected and shared with third parties.

items.title: Progress Patches Critical MOVEit Automation Bug Enabling Authentication Bypass
items.description: Progress Software has released updates to address two security flaws in MOVEit Automation, including a critical bug that could result in an authentication bypass. MOVEit Automation (formerly Central) is a secure, server-based managed file transfer (MFT) solution used to schedule and automate file movement workflows in enterprise environments without requiring any custom scripts.  The

items.title: Ransomware group claims breach of pro-Orbán Hungarian media firm
items.description: Mediaworks confirmed the incident on Friday, warning that “a significant amount of illegally obtained data may have come into the possession of unauthorized persons."

items.title: Silver Fox Springs Tax-Themed Attacks on Orgs in India, Russia
items.description: More than 1,600 socially engineered messages from the China-backed advanced persistent threat (APT) group target various sectors to deliver the previously undocumented ABCDoor backdoor, ValleyRAT, and other malware.

items.title: DShield Honeypot Update, (Mon, May 4th)
items.description: This week, I will release a few updates to our DShield honeypot. The update should happen automatically if you have "automatic updates" enabled on your system. There will be two major changes:

items.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: AI-Powered Phishing, Android Spying Tool, Linux Exploit, GitHub RCE & More
items.description: This week, the shadows moved faster than the patches. While most teams were still triaging last month’s alerts, attackers had already turned control panels into kill switches, kernels into open doors, and open-source pipelines into silent delivery systems. The game has shifted from breach to occupation. They’re living inside SaaS sessions, pushing code with trusted commits, and scaling

items.title: How Dark Reading Lifted Off the Launchpad in 2006
items.description: Twenty years ago, this media brand didn't have a print edition to attract eyeballs and sponsors. Top-notch content and editorial talent did the heavy lifting.

items.description: Wireshark release 4.6.5 fixes 43 vulnerabilities (38 CVEs) and 35 bugs.

items.title: 76% of All Crypto Stolen in 2026 Is Now in North Korea
items.description: North Korean threat actors are pulling off historic cryptocurrency heists on a yearly, sometimes weekly basis now. AI might be helping them.

items.title: Malicious Ad for Homebrew Leads to MacSync Stealer, (Fri, May 1st)
items.description: Introduction

items.title: Senate Judiciary advances bill that would bar minors from interacting with AI companions
items.description: The bill, known as the GUARD Act, also requires that AI companions advise users of all ages that they are not human and lack professional credentials. It also makes it a crime for AI companions to knowingly ask kids for sexual content or to produce it.

items.title: If AI's So Smart, Why Does It Keep Deleting Production Databases?
items.description: The issue isn't artificial intelligence, but rather an industry adding AI agent integrations into production environments before proper security testing.

items.title: Name That Toon: Mark of (Security) Progress
items.description: Feeling creative? Have something to say about the last 20 years of cybersecurity? Our editors will award the best cybersecurity-related caption with a $20 gift card.

items.title: CISA, US and International Partners Release Guide to Secure Adoption of Agentic AI
20 Years in Cyber: Dark Reading Marks Milestone With Month of Special Coverage
items.description: On this day in 2006, Dark Reading went live. We have a celebration planned that spans our two decades of covering the industry, and you, dear readers, are invited.

items.title: Anti-DDoS Firm Heaped Attacks on Brazilian ISPs
items.description: A Brazilian tech firm that specializes in protecting networks from distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks has been enabling a botnet responsible for an extended campaign of massive DDoS attacks against other network operators in Brazil, KrebsOnSecurity has learned. The firm's chief executive says the malicious activity resulted from a security breach and was likely the work of a competitor trying to tarnish his company's public image.

items.title: ISC Stormcast For Thursday, April 30th, 2026 https://isc.sans.edu/podcastdetail/9912, (Thu, Apr 30th)
items.description: (c) SANS Internet Storm Center. https://isc.sans.edu Creative Commons Attribution-Noncommercial 3.0 United States License.

items.title: CISA and U.S. Government Partners Unveil Guide to Accelerate Zero Trust Adoption in Operational Technology
CISA, National Cyber Security Centre (NCSC) UK, and Global Partners Issue Advisory on Chinese Government-Linked Covert Cyber Networks
CISA Warns of FIRESTARTER Malware Targeting Cisco ASA including Firepower and Secure Firewall Products
‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
items.description: A 24-year-old British national and senior member of the cybercrime group "Scattered Spider" has pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Tyler Robert Buchanan admitted his role in a series of text-message phishing attacks in the summer of 2022 that allowed the group to hack into at least a dozen major technology companies and steal tens of millions of dollars worth of cryptocurrency from investors.

items.title: Patch Tuesday, April 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft today pushed software updates to fix a staggering 167 security vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and related software, including a SharePoint Server zero-day and a publicly disclosed weakness in Windows Defender dubbed "BlueHammer." Separately, Google Chrome fixed its fourth zero-day of 2026, and an emergency update for Adobe Reader nixes an actively exploited flaw that can lead to remote code execution.

items.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
items.description: Hackers linked to Russia's military intelligence units are using known flaws in older Internet routers to mass harvest authentication tokens from Microsoft Office users, security experts warned today. The spying campaign allowed state-backed Russian hackers to quietly siphon authentication tokens from users on more than 18,000 networks without deploying any malicious software or code.

items.title: Germany Doxes “UNKN,” Head of RU Ransomware Gangs REvil, GandCrab
items.description: An elusive hacker who went by the handle "UNKN" and ran the early Russian ransomware groups GandCrab and REvil now has a name and a face. Authorities in Germany say 31-year-old Russian Daniil Maksimovich Shchukin headed both cybercrime gangs and helped carry out at least 130 acts of computer sabotage and extortion against victims across the country between 2019 and 2021.

items.title: ‘CanisterWorm’ Springs Wiper Attack Targeting Iran
items.description: A financially motivated data theft and extortion group is attempting to inject itself into the Iran war, unleashing a worm that spreads through poorly secured cloud services and wipes data on infected systems that use Iran's time zone or have Farsi set as the default language.

items.title: Feds Disrupt IoT Botnets Behind Huge DDoS Attacks
items.description: The U.S. Justice Department joined authorities in Canada and Germany in dismantling the online infrastructure behind four highly disruptive botnets that compromised more than three million hacked Internet of Things (IoT) devices, such as routers and web cameras. The feds say the four botnets -- named Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid and Mossad -- are responsible for a series of recent record-smashing distributed denial-of-service (DDoS) attacks capable of knocking nearly any target offline.

items.title: Iran-Backed Hackers Claim Wiper Attack on Medtech Firm Stryker
items.description: A hacktivist group with links to Iran's intelligence agencies is claiming responsibility for a data-wiping attack against Stryker, a global medical technology company based in Michigan. News reports out of Ireland, Stryker's largest hub outside of the United States, said the company sent home more than 5,000 workers there today. Meanwhile, a voicemail message at Stryker's main U.S. headquarters says the company is currently experiencing a building emergency.

items.title: Microsoft Patch Tuesday, March 2026 Edition
items.description: Microsoft Corp. today pushed security updates to fix at least 77 vulnerabilities in its Windows operating systems and other software. There are no pressing "zero-day" flaws this month (compared to February's five zero-day treat), but as usual some patches may deserve more rapid attention from organizations using Windows. Here are a few highlights from this month's Patch Tuesday.

items.title: How AI Assistants are Moving the Security Goalposts
items.description: AI-based assistants or "agents" -- autonomous programs that have access to the user's computer, files, online services and can automate virtually any task -- are growing in popularity with developers and IT workers. But as so many eyebrow-raising headlines over the past few weeks have shown, these powerful and assertive new tools are rapidly shifting the security priorities for organizations, while blurring the lines between data and code, trusted co-worker and insider threat, ninja hacker and novice code jockey.

items.title: CISA Issues Updated RESURGE Malware Analysis Highlighting a Stealthy but Active Threat
Immediate Action Required: CISA Issues Emergency Directive to Secure Cisco SD-WAN Systems
CISA Announces New Town Halls to Engage with Stakeholders on Cyber Incident Reporting for Critical Infrastructure
CISA’s 2025 Year in Review: Driving Security and Resilience Across Critical Infrastructure
CISA Releases Guide to Help Critical Infrastructure Users Adopt More Secure Communication
Student Loan Breach Exposes 2.5M Records
items.description: 2.5 million people were affected, in a breach that could spell more trouble down the line.

items.title: Watering Hole Attacks Push ScanBox Keylogger
items.description: Researchers uncover a watering hole attack likely carried out by APT TA423, which attempts to plant the ScanBox JavaScript-based reconnaissance tool.

items.title: Tentacles of ‘0ktapus’ Threat Group Victimize 130 Firms
items.description: Over 130 companies tangled in sprawling phishing campaign that spoofed a multi-factor authentication system.

items.title: Ransomware Attacks are on the Rise
items.description: Lockbit is by far this summer’s most prolific ransomware group, trailed by two offshoots of the Conti group.

items.title: Cybercriminals Are Selling Access to Chinese Surveillance Cameras
items.description: Tens of thousands of cameras have failed to patch a critical, 11-month-old CVE, leaving thousands of organizations exposed.

items.title: Twitter Whistleblower Complaint: The TL;DR Version
items.description: Twitter is blasted for security and privacy lapses by the company’s former head of security who alleges the social media giant’s actions amount to a national security risk.

items.title: Firewall Bug Under Active Attack Triggers CISA Warning
items.description: CISA is warning that Palo Alto Networks’ PAN-OS is under active attack and needs to be patched ASAP.

items.title: Fake Reservation Links Prey on Weary Travelers
items.description: Fake travel reservations are exacting more pain from the travel weary, already dealing with the misery of canceled flights and overbooked hotels.

items.title: iPhone Users Urged to Update to Patch 2 Zero-Days
items.description: Separate fixes to macOS and iOS patch respective flaws in the kernel and WebKit that can allow threat actors to take over devices and are under attack.

items.title: Google Patches Chrome’s Fifth Zero-Day of the Year
items.description: An insufficient validation input flaw, one of 11 patched in an update this week, could allow for arbitrary code execution and is under active attack.
